candidates.stmt:
It’s time for a local voice that listens.
​Hi everyone, I’m Carrie Gledhill, and I’m proud to announce I am running to be your local councillor for Airedale & Ferry Fryston. I live in this ward and was raised in this ward, I attended oyster park from nursery to juniors then onto Airedale high.
​This isn’t just about politics for me it’s about our home. We’ve all seen our streets overlooked for too long, and I believe we deserve better.
​I’m not here to make grand promises and disappear. I’m here to work for my neighbours. Whether it’s fly-tipping on your corner or the state of our local play areas, I want to hear what matters to you.
​👇 What is the number one thing you’d like to see changed in our ward? Let me know in the comments.
​#Airedale #FerryFryston #LocalVoice #CommunityFirst

I’m Adam Freer standing for you in Airedale and Ferry Fryston for Reform. 
I’m a father of 4 wonderful kids and a Cas lad born and bred. I grew up on Council estates in social houses part of a single parent family. We were stuck scraping by on welfare my entire childhood.  Often we had to choose between electricity or food each week, I truly know what it’s like to struggle to get by and how tough it can be. 
My family has called Airedale home for coming into two decades now and it is the hotbed of homes in the town. 
I currently work for the NHS, before that Central Government, and even the best part of a decade for the Department for a Work and a Pensions. Most of my working time so far has been serving the country and the local population, supporting the vulnerable and helping them rebuild their lives.  Standing for Council to serve my neighbours is second nature to me so I’m not a novice in doing what’s right for the people. 
I have seen how Wakefield Council have let where we live fall behind other areas in the district. Labour have let us down to prioritise vanity projects like tens of millions to buy The Ridings, or give away our buildings for £1. 
At the moment Wakefield Council serves only itself and what Westminster whispers to it. They rely on you to not ask questions or care where your money goes and take away your parks, leave your roads to ruin let crime run riot and take away your kids safe spaces. It has to stop. 
On May 7th you have three votes.
Remember;
Adam, Carrie, Dion
Its time for Reform

Hi everyone, I’m Dion Lowe and I’m really excited to be running in the local elections this May to be your local councillor for Airedale & Ferry Fryston. 
I grew up in Altofts, moving to Normanton as a teenager and then over to Castleford for the majority of my adult life. I have lived in the Airedale & Ferry Fryston ward for the last 7 years. 
I have been successful in business and I spent over 10 years as a Director at Castleford Tigers a privilege that was rewarded to me by the late Jack Fulton. 
This is my first venture into politics and I’m ready to listen and support everyone across the community. 
Having a local voice addressing local issues has to be a priority for us all.
I will work tirelessly to represent you and your family, delivering better services, safer streets and a neighbourhood that we can all be proud of. 
It’s time for change, it’s time for REFORM. 
Vote x3 on May 7th 🗳️🗳️🗳️
​#airedale  #ferryfryston  #townville #Localvoice  #saferneighbourhood

Hi, I’m Dan, a Maths Teacher who’s lived in Wakefield for over 7 years now. Born and raised in Leeds, I’ve spent time in Hull, and New Zealand, before settling in Wakefield with my girlfriend. I am a keen football, rugby league and cricket fan, and like to keep active with walking, running, swimming and cycling.

I have become increasingly aware of the responsibility we all have to take what action we can to save our communities, and the planet, from reaching a critical point of no return. I am keen to take what steps I can as an individual to lessen my ‘footprint’, such as; eating a plant based diet, minimising new purchases in favour of second hand, recycling everything suitable, thinking consciously before buying anything, and making eco based swaps around the home as and when needed.

I joined the Green Party because I wanted my politics to be honest, well intentioned, and fighting the good fight for the community, and for the sake of the planet.

In Wakefield and the Five Towns, there is a wide scope for improvements to existing plans, and limiting the environmental impact we all feel in our communities. A Green Councillor would hold the Labour majority Council to account on the climate emergency declared. We would work to increase the delivery of clean green renewable energy, and work together with local transport suppliers to improve city wide provision.

My name is Michelle Keeley.
I am standing as your Reform UK candidate for Whitwood and Altofts.
Although I’m originally from Northumberland (yes – a Geordie!), I have lived in West Yorkshire for over 22 years and in Whitwood for the past eight. This is my home.
Why I’m standing
After years of Labour control, Wakefield is in a mess. There is no respect. The NHS is broken, local opportunities have declined, costs have risen, crime and antisocial behaviour have increased, our once thriving towns are unrecognisable and council debt has soared with little accountability.
Our communities deserve better. Our communities deserve the truth!
My experience
I have worked in education, early years and care, business, and law and order. I spent 12 years with the police, most recently as an investigator in serious and complex crime within safeguarding. I have also been a voluntary advocate for those who had no voice. I have been involved in legal cases regarding negligence in the NHS and care sector, and discrimination in the workplace.
Keeping women, children and vulnerable people safe is not a slogan for me – it’s my career.
As a cancer survivor and the mother of an adult daughter who is neurodiverse, I have seen first hand how hard people struggle when public services fail them. The NHS, Police and Social care are buckling under pressure. Too many hardworking and vulnerable residents are literally having to fight for the support that they need. 
That isn’t fair.
If elected, I will:
• Oppose the use of migrant hotels and HMO’s, prioritising community safety
• Protect women and girls
• Ensure there is no queue jumping for services including housing – put our residents first!
• Push for firm action on crime, shoplifting and antisocial behaviour
• Support young people with joined up local services, so they thrive in safe environments
• Champion local businesses and growth
• Be a voice for our vulnerable, elderly and veterans
• Hold the council to account for debt and waste, saving

John is a hardworking, dedicated and effective communicator, committed to transparency, honesty and ethical conduct. With a 25-year career supporting the NHS, ensuring that hospitals are equipped to manage the needs of its patients across England and Wales, needless to say the last four years have been the most testing of his career to date.

He grew up in Wakefield and attended Kettlethorpe High School, followed by A-Levels at Wakefield College. After numerous temporary jobs, including his first job collecting glasses in Legends and Harleys in Castleford at 17-years old, he started working at NHS Supply Chain in Normanton in 1998, where he still works as an Inventory Manager.

John has stood for election for several years in Altofts and Whitwood and has been honoured to lead the only Independent party in the Wakefield District. He believes that independence from a party whip is critical to the accountability of an MP to the people he or she represents. With no party whip, Reform UK MPs are accountable only to the constituents and not to big business or big pharma lobbyists.

He is only interested in serving the people of Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley, not his own political ambitions.

As a part of the Vote Leave campaign, which won 70% of the votes in Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley in 2016, John knows what it means to fight against the establishment… and win!

John has been working with a blockchain developer to develop the UKs first Direct Democracy portal, BlocVote. This will allow people to directly instruct their elected representative of their views, opinions and ultimately how they would like their MP to vote on the most critical of issues to impact our lives. With this system comes the right of Recall. If you are not happy with the performance of your MP…. sack them. No ifs, no buts, they are not doing as they promised they would and are not listening to you… sack them.

In a direct democracy, power is not concentrated in the hands of a sele

My name is Jamie McGrevy.
I am proud to have served both my country and my local community.

Having joined the Armed Forces at the age of 18, I was proud to serve my country.
After leaving the forces I then went on to serve local people in my role as a Police Officer.

This life experience has given me a clear understanding of what real service means and it is why I have decided to stand and represent my local community.

I believe our area has suffered from a lack of effective policing for far too long now and over time, we have seen a clear decline in crucial front line policing, policing where it really matters.

After years of neglect and proper investment by both former and current governments, this needs to change so that local people feel safe in their own communities.

Given my background, I understand the importance of strong, visible policing.

I am committed to making sure that Altofts and Whitwood are properly represented and by working alongside my Reform UK colleagues I will give residents a stronger voice.

I have witnessed the impact of current housing policies, including the use of affordable housing, local hotels, bed and breakfasts and HMOs in our area to accommodate illegal immigrants.

This situation is placing strain on local residents and this must be addressed so that those people who need it can have access to affordable housing. People deserve to have quiet enjoyment of their homes without worrying about who is living on their doorstep illegally.

As a veteran, I am particularly passionate about ensuring that those who have served our country are properly supported. Veterans should never be overlooked after serving their country.

For too long, the villages of Altofts and Whitwood have been let down by Wakefield Council. I will stand firmly for transparency, accountability and responsible use of public funds.

It’s time for change and I am committed to delivering it.

I have lived in Wakefield all my life and have seen a decline in our communities.
I have spent my working life providing legal services to clients and firmly believing that they should get excellent service and value for money.
If elected, I would do my utmost to ensure that this principle is applied, when spending your hard earned money and seek to provide the things that the people of Castleford actually want.

I am a retired accountant and have plenty of spare time to help Wakefield improve its services to the public.
As Finance Director at two major organisations I was responsible for making efficiencies to bring down costs and reduce waste.
If elected I will endeavour to introduce sound financial management to Wakefield Council.

Brett is a 20+ year Army veteran, his career has taken him all over the world. Brett grew up in Airedale, Castleford and from a young age it was clear he cared passionately about his country.
Joining the Army was Brett's way of helping to preserve the British way of life and that passion lives on today and that is why he is standing as your candidate, so he can continue to preserve the culture and future of this great country we are so proud to call Great Britain.
But Brett needs your help to achieve this goal. Like Brett, you can help preserve our local community and Country by putting your X by the side of Brett's name. Together we can and will make a difference.
Crime in the Castleford area over the years has been on the increase. Respect and discipline have been on the decline in our area.
Theft is affecting our local businesses and the knock-on effect impacts all of us. Anti-social behaviour is becoming a big problem and property theft has a huge impact on the victims.
Here are a few ideas that can change this;
CCTV plays a very important role.
Investing in new equipment and strategic placement will reassure our residents and deter criminals.
By making policing more visible, this will work hand in hand with CCTV, helping to direct officers where they are needed more quickly.
People say we need to get the youth off the streets, the best way to do this is to give them somewhere to go, give them a purpose and an interest. Engage with the youth not dismiss them.
Take pride in where we live, working together as a community to install pride, respect, discipline and getting to know each other will give us all an insight to life from someone else’s perspective.
Help me to help you, make Castleford Central and Glasshoughton not just any community, let's make it our community.
Vote Reform.

I’m standing because I believe local people deserve someone who turns up, listens, and gets things sorted.

I live close by, so this isn’t abstract for me. The roads, pavements, local services, and safety issues you deal with are the same ones I see every day. When something needs reporting or chasing, I’ll do it, and I’ll follow it through.

I’m not here to make noise for the sake of it. I’m here to make progress where it actually matters.

-I will be **visible and available**, not just at election time
-I will **stand up for residents and local businesses**, and push for fair outcomes
-I will focus on **cleaner, safer streets**, including potholes, pavements, and fly-tipping
-I will work to improve **health, safety, and day-to-day living standards** across the area

I’ve already spent time speaking to residents and reporting issues, and I’ve seen first-hand that persistence gets results. That’s the approach I’ll carry on with.

If you want someone who will take responsibility, keep you informed, and get stuck in, I’d value your vote.

My name is Billy Garbutt and I’ve lived, schooled and worked in this town my entire life. From my first days in the mines after leaving school to the various supervisory roles I’ve held since, I’ve spent my career leading men and getting the job done. Now, I want to do that for you.
I’m standing for Reform UK because I’m tired of seeing our town become a shell of what it once was under years of Labour leadership. I want to give our "Knottla" kids the same sense of purpose and opportunity I had growing up, safe streets, places to learn and a future to look forward to.
It’s time to put local people first, restore law and order and ensure our young people aren't overlooked for housing in their own community.
On May 7th, vote for change.
Vote for a local who fights for you.

My name is Lisa Marie Frost and I am very proud to be a candidate for Knottingley and Ferrybridge.
Knottingley has been a huge part of my life from bringing my kids to the swimming pool a bit of shopping, dinner in the cafe and also working in Knottingley Town Hall.
I love visiting my friends and family on the fantastic estate of the Warwick. Knottingley and Ferrybridge is full of hardworking and proud people, who are proud of their homes and stand together and I will stand with them. Not as a voice on the phone or a name behind a generated email but in person when you need me. If it matters to you it matters to me, so let’s get Knottingley and Ferrybridge what they deserve.

Hi, I’m David Kennedy. I’ve spent many years working in Knottingley & Ferrybridge, from my early days at ExPac and Bagleys as a teenager, to roles at William Hill on both Knottingley (where my mother was the manager) & Ferrybridge offices and eventually as a manager at Hutton’s before it went into liquidation.
These experiences have given me a strong connection to the area and a clear understanding of the challenges it faces.
Over time, I’ve witnessed a steady decline not only in this area but across the wider Wakefield district as a whole. High streets have struggled, opportunities have diminished and places we cherished and have fond memories of are gone forever. Like many of you, I’ve felt frustrated watching this happen, knowing our communities deserve better.
That’s why I made the decision to stop sitting on the side-lines and take action. I’m not doing this for the sake of being a councillor or for a pay check I want to be part of the change our area needs, working to restore pride, create opportunities and build a stronger future for both current residents and the next generation. I believe in practical solutions, honest communication and putting local people first.
My background in customer relations and marketing has taught me how to listen, adapt, deliver results and promote our area, skills I’m committed to using to benefit our community. I care deeply about this area because it has shaped who I am and I want to help shape a more positive future for it.
This May, I’m asking for your support. Together, we can begin to turn things around and create a place we’re proud to call home again.

Adam Belcher is a local resident who is campaigning to improve transport links in the Crofton, Ryhill and Walton area. He is also keen to defend local services from cuts, and to promote apprenticeships as a path for young people.

Adam has lived in Crofton all his life, having attended Crofton Academy and Barnsley College. He currently studies Computer Science at the University of Birmingham. He is also an author.

"I worry that our community is being left behind. Big cities such as Leeds are receiving lots of funding and are growing rapidly. Young people in our area are missing out on these opportunities because we're not connected."

The local transport situation:

"As a student at Barnsley College, the bus from Crofton ran once an hour or less, and it was often badly delayed. It was a complete nightmare. At university in Birmingham, I have seen how much better transport is in other parts of the country. Trains and buses in the West Midlands run more regularly and on-time. This is clear proof that our area has been left behind. Meanwhile, cities like Leeds and Sheffield are due to be connected by HS2, whilst our area only sees the negatives of HS2 - this isn't fair.

The Wakefield-Doncaster railway line runs through our area. Local trains stop at Sandal and at Fitzwilliam, but Crofton is ignored. Our train station has been closed since the 1950s. Perhaps it is time to ask - should we re-open a station at Crofton? We would love to hear what you think."

I am committed to being your local champion, fighting for real, no-nonsense change and putting our residents first.
Pontefract North deserves a new, positive direction, rather than more of the same.
Let’s work together to build something better for Pontefract and better for Wakefield.

A proud Yorkshireman, I have lived across many 
parts of Yorkshire keeping close to my roots.

I am extremely passionate about ensuring everyone has good life 
chances, this starts with Education and have invested time and energy with four schools in the local area as a school governor.

I am a huge believer in apprentices 
as every person needs a foot in the door.

We need to see the local council encourage new businesses that will 
give people good quality well paid jobs not just lots of warehouses.

I am committed to reducing waste and using taxpayers’ money efficiently to deliver the best services.
I will answer your questions honestly and, if I don’t know, I will find out.
I am dedicated to working hard for the best outcomes for our community in Pontefract.

Pot holes, traffic lights, housing developments and congestion on the A639 and still no relief road. I fought against closure of PGI and Labour's mission to build PFIs.
The Hermitage has been in danger of flooding ever since and I’m against empty buildings used as Multi Occupational Housing.

We moved to Pontefract to be close to family, a place we now call home. My motivation comes from my faith, which challenges me to serve and put others first.
A promise I can make is to serve with integrity and diligence using skills acquired over 43 years in business to make a difference.

I have lived in Pontefract most of my life and as a retired businessman, I know the value of every pound.
I’ll push for responsible spending, challenge costly vanity projects, support local jobs and local businesses, protect key services and always speak up for residents’ concerns.

What one thing do you think Wakefield District Council has to change it’s approach towards?

Recognising that green words need green actions. By supporting a green positive agenda, the WMDC could attract investment, create jobs, and help save the planet.

What difference do you think a Green Party Councillor could make in Castleford Central & Glasshoughton?

A Green Party councillor would offer differing views and perspectives from those of Labour and the Conservatives. Wakefield Council seems trapped in a perpetual cycle of doing the same things and hoping for different outcomes. New opinions and a broad church of political views would foster new ideas and new ways of doing things to help the people of Castleford Central & Glasshoughton ward.

What five words (or phrases) best describe you as a prospective councillor?

Motivated to built a better future.

How would a Green approach help ordinary people to better manage the cost of living?

The UK has some of the least green housing in the developed world. Homes are rarely well-insulated and are inevitably heated by fossil fuels. A green approach to housing upgrades will make homes warmer and cheaper to run. Similarly, a switch away from fossil fuels to renewables is proven to lower energy costs and make household bills cheaper. Lastly, a strong push to take public control of privatised resources such as utilities and public transport will mean that private companies are not able to make massive profits that put shareholders before consumers.

I have lived in Wakefield East for 20 years, and work locally with people with disabilities. Wakefield Council has a massive majority of Labour Councillors, and no Green Councillors presently. I would be free to challenge, bring fresh ideas and promote a voice for local ecology. Key issues for me include; the cuts to the adult social care budget, the amount of plastic waste flowing in the Calder which is clear to see at Stanley Ferry and flows along and through our ward, the state of our roads paths and cycle ways, and the issue of Child Safeguarding which has recently been flagged up by OFSTED as having significant weaknesses. Please get in touch.

I am standing to deliver clear, measurable results for our community.

I live in this ward. I face the same issues as you. I am a father to a young child and a son to an elderly parent. I understand the pressure on families, services and daily life.

I run businesses across different sectors. I manage budgets. I solve problems. I am also a law student aspiring to be a barrister. I bring discipline, focus and accountability to everything I do.

Listening to you. Working for you. Delivering for our community.

My priorities

1. Fix council finances. Cut waste. Take control.
    Your money must be used properly. I will challenge waste and improve transparency.

2. Clean up and restore pride in our city centre.
    A clean, safe centre supports jobs and growth. I will push for higher standards.

3. Support local businesses and revive our high streets.
    You need a strong local economy. I will back traders and bring life back to our town centre.

4. Build homes with infrastructure in place first.
    New housing must include roads, schools and services. I will not support poor planning.

5. Protect neighbourhoods from overdevelopment.
    You deserve balanced communities. I will stand against harmful planning decisions.

6. Fix roads and tackle potholes properly.
    You should not pay the cost of poor roads. I will push for long term solutions.

7. Crack down on crime and anti social behaviour.
    You deserve to feel safe. I will support stronger enforcement and action.

8. Improve transport reliability and access.
    Getting around should be simple. I will work to improve transport for everyone.

9. Strengthen adult social care.
    You and your family deserve dignity and support. I will push for better services.

10. Stand up for residents and businesses effected by the Ridings Demolition.
    I will represent you and make sure your voice is heard.

What you can expect

Clear communication
Focus on results
Accountability for every decision
Consistent action

I wil

I am the only candidate standing in this election that actually lives in Horbury, infact I have lived in Horbury all my life. I know how much the Labour controlled council treat both Horbury and Ossett as the forgotten part of the District . I now that our ward needs someone to stand up in the Council Chamber and fight for our area rather than being told how to vote by their party bosses .

Having built a multimillion pound lubricant business with production in the UK and Europe and growing sales around the world, mark now wants to engage with our local community and try and make a difference. He moved to Chapelthorpe in 2018 and having sold half the business to his staff, he has the time and the energy to engage in local politics to help our community. Mark has grown increasingly concerned with how consecutive Governments and their ill thought out policies and incompetent delivery continues to weaken our country. After the disastrous few years of Tory rule it really is time to question the political landscape and recognise that the Liberal Democrats stand for a fairer and balanced society and should be a viable alternative to the two party system that has got to change.

I am Nadeem Ahmed - the Conservative candidate in the upcoming by election on 23 June.

I was born in Manygates Hospital, the eldest son of immigrant parents. I grew up in Agbrigg, Wakefield with my family and attended Sandal Magna First School, Manygates Middle School and St Thomas a Becket Comprehensive School. I come from a humble working class background with a strong work ethic and a belief that hard work and perseverance can lead to success.

The two previous MPs failed Wakefield and on top of that we have a Labour council which has also failed the residents of Wakefield.

It's time for change in Wakefield - it's time for a local MP who has a vested interest in Wakefield which goes beyond politics and straight into the hearts and souls of our vibrant and diverse communities. A strong local voice who has a proven track record of getting things done having been a local councillor for 16 years.

That's why I'm asking you to vote for me, NADEEM AHMED, on 23 June so that I can be YOUR voice in parliament and raise the issues that matter to YOU.

David was born and raised in the West Riding of Yorkshire, studied Civil Engineering at London University, has lived in the Wrenthorpe and Outwood West ward of Wakefield since 1986, and is married with grown-up children.

As a Chartered Civil Engineer, designing roads, bridges, drainage and flood defences, he has worked locally in Yorkshire and overseas in Africa and the Middle East where he ran the office of a UK company with a multinational staff of around 80.

He is a member of the local community association and environmental group, and a churchwarden.

He became involved with politics in 2010, joining UKIP and after Brexit moved to Reform UK. He has stood as a candidate in Euro, Westminster, and local elections, and for 4 years he served his ward as a UKIP councillor on Wakefield MDC, repeatedly challenging the Labour Council leadership on planning issues, the use of Council funds, and was the only councillor to oppose the privatisation of Council services and support union members against the move.

At a national level the Conservatives have let us down, but Labour do not seem any better, which is affecting us all locally. The dramatic population increase without corresponding increases in housing and infrastructure is causing hardship and frustration through high housing costs and long waits for services. David has seen how other countries deal with control of those needed to work in their economies – it can be done. Meanwhile the obsessive drive for “Net Zero” puts our prosperity in jeopardy.

David is standing for Reform UK because he has had enough of the mediocre representation over the last few decades that has led us to where we are today.

If you are fed up too, get involved, join us, volunteer and help us campaign.
